classification.plain_english: A test took longer than the allowed time and was killed. This may indicate a genuine timeout in the code under test, a locked resource, or a network issue.
common_causes: The code under test genuinely times out (e.g. a slow database query)
The test machine is under load and the timeout is too tight
A resource (DB connection, API endpoint) is not available
An infinite loop or deadlock in the code under test
suggested_checks: Run the test in isolation with `mix test test/example_app_web/user_auth_test.exs:12`
Check if the timeout value is appropriate for the environment
Look for any recent changes to the code under test
Verify external services (DB, API) are reachable

failures.name: test create user with duplicate email

classification.plain_english: An ExUnit assertion failed. The test expected a value but found a different one.
common_causes: The code under test has a bug
Test data is not in the expected state
A recent refactor changed the expected behavior
suggested_checks: Run the specific test: `mix test test/example_app/accounts_test.exs:8`
Check the failure message for the actual vs expected values
Look at the test setup to ensure data is correct
Check for recent changes to the Accounts context

slow.name: test create user with valid attrs
